task_completion.description: The BFI25 dataset is loaded into Jamovi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi application window is open and displays the BFI25 dataset, with columns such as A1, A2, and gender visible in the spreadsheet view.

task_completion.description: A computed variable 'A1r' is created to reverse-code item A1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column named 'A1r' is visible in the Jamovi data spreadsheet. The formula '7 - A1' should ideally be visible in the formula editor.

task_completion.description: A computed variable 'Agreeableness' is created to calculate the scale mean.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column named 'Agreeableness' is visible in the Jamovi data spreadsheet. The formula 'MEAN(A1r, A2, A3, A4, A5)' should ideally be visible in the formula editor.

task_completion.description: An Independent Samples T-Test is conducted comparing 'Agreeableness' between genders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results pane displays an 'Independent Samples T-Test' table with 'Agreeableness' listed as the dependent variable.

task_completion.description: Group descriptives are included in the T-Test output.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results pane displays a 'Group Descriptives' table showing the mean, median, etc., for 'Agreeableness' split by the 'gender' grouping variable (levels 1 and 2).

task_completion.description: The Jamovi project is saved as 'BFI25_Agreeableness.omv'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi window title bar displays 'BFI25_Agreeableness.omv', or a file manager/terminal shows the file saved at '/home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/BFI25_Agreeableness.omv'.

task_completion.description: The required statistics are recorded in a text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, or cat output in terminal) displays the contents of 'agreeableness_results.txt', containing the mean for males (gender=1), mean for females (gender=2), the t-statistic, and the p-value, all rounded to 3 decimals.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi graphical user interface to perform the data manipulation and statistical analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jamovi GUI (menus, formula editor, analysis options) rather than terminal commands executing R or Python scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The results recorded in the text file match the output generated in Jamovi.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into 'agreeableness_results.txt' correspond to the values visible in the Jamovi results pane, indicating genuine transcription rather than hallucination or hardcoding.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create the text file using a script with hardcoded expected values.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual evidence of the agent actually running the T-Test in Jamovi before creating and populating the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected means are roughly 4.376 for males (gender=1) and 4.776 for females (gender=2). The t-statistic should be around -10.39 (Student's) or -10.09 (Welch's), and the p-value should be < .001. The dataset is located at /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/BFI25.csv, the project should be saved to /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/BFI25_Agreeableness.omv, and the results text file to /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/agreeableness_results.txt.
